# S004. Jump Diffusion Crash Filter

## Positioning

This candidate is not trying to predict every move; it is trying to keep the book alive when jumps make the normal assumptions wrong. Think of it as a crash filter and exposure governor rather than a pure alpha engine.

## Reference Basis

- [Parameter Estimation of the Heston Volatility Model with Jumps in the Asset Prices](../../references/papers/2211.14814_heston_jumps.pdf) - jump-risk calibration reference
- [Mean Reversion Trading with Sequential Deadlines and Transaction Costs](../../references/papers/1707.03498_mean_reversion_deadlines.pdf) - deadline and transaction-cost discipline

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### HFT and Intraday Stack

Replay-backed intraday ingestion and synthetic matching for jump filters and short-horizon routing.
- [src/ingestion/websocket_client.py](../../../src/ingestion/websocket_client.py) - stream/replay event ingestion
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - intraday queue-aware matching
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - stream freshness and heartbeat supervision

### Market Data and Features

Point-in-time data loading plus the shared feature builder used by most signal stacks.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - point-in-time market data loading
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - technical, lagged, and derived feature construction

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use gap features, overnight returns, and intraday range statistics from the market-data path.
- Replay or stream intraday inputs through `src/ingestion/websocket_client.py` when available.
- Keep the filter aligned with the same state and freshness checks enforced by the health monitor.

## Execution Path

- Detect jump probability and immediately haircut exposure when the threshold trips.
- Use the crash filter to pause liquidity taking or widen the entry threshold.
- Replay the filter with queue-aware synthetic fills so the slippage effect is visible.

## Risk Controls

- Prefer false negatives over false positives only when the opportunity cost is explicit; otherwise stay conservative.
- Hard-stop trading if the stream or heartbeat breaks during a jump cluster.
- Do not let the filter become a de facto signal generator; its job is to defend capital.

## Validation Gates

- Measure event-day drawdown, slippage, and false-positive rate on the captured replay fixture.
- Check whether the filter cuts risk on crash days without flattening normal periods.
- Compare against a simple volatility spike heuristic to avoid unnecessary complexity.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if it only helps in hindsight.
- Archive if the filter reduces too much alpha in normal markets.
- Archive if intraday data quality is not good enough to keep the classifier honest.

## Next Step

Use this as the crash-risk overlay for S021 and F027.
